name: Transtheoretical Model (Stages of Change)
description: Maps where someone is in their change journey across 5 stages. Determines which interventions are appropriate at each stage.
when_to_use: User is at a different readiness level than the task demands
Orchestrator needs to calibrate how aggressively to push toward a goal
User keeps asking about a topic but not acting on advice
Agent needs to determine appropriate intervention intensity

precontemplation.description: Not yet considering change. May not see a problem.
signals: User hasn't asked for help with this area
appropriate_actions: Ask open-ended questions, don't push solutions
Share relevant data without prescribing action

contemplation.description: Aware of the issue, thinking about change but not committed.
appropriate_actions: Explore pros and cons of change
Use Socratic questioning to deepen awareness
Help envision what change would look like

preparation.description: Committed to change, planning how to do it.
appropriate_actions: Identify potential obstacles and plan for them
Connect to relevant frameworks and tools

maintenance.description: Change is established, focus is on sustaining it.
signals: User has been consistent for a period
Focus shifts from 'how to start' to 'how to keep going'
appropriate_actions: Identify and plan for relapse triggers

stage_detection_prompts: Has the user taken any concrete action toward this goal, or are they still exploring?
Is the user asking 'should I?' (contemplation) or 'how do I?' (preparation/action)?
How long has the user been working on this? Are they establishing or maintaining?
What level of specificity is the user requesting?
